auth_notes: Requires creating a developer app in the Jobber Developer Center to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret. n8n users must configure a 'Generic OAuth2 API' credential with the correct authorization and access token URLs, plus specific scopes.
rate_limits: 10 requests per second (burst) and 500 requests per minute per app/account.
gotchas: Jobber uses GraphQL exclusively, meaning standard REST path-based requests won't work; you must write query/mutation strings.
The OAuth 2.0 flow requires a public-facing redirect URL, which can be tricky for local n8n instances.
Pagination in GraphQL uses cursors (edges/node pattern), which requires more complex looping logic in n8n than simple page numbers.
client_must_provide: Jobber Developer Center App Credentials (Client ID, Client Secret)
Jobber Account Admin access for initial OAuth authorization
operations_available: Subscribe to Webhooks for record changes
research_notes: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node set to POST against 'https://api.getjobber.com/api/graphql'. The payload must be a JSON object containing a 'query' string. Authentication is best handled by n8n's built-in OAuth2 credential type to manage token refreshing automatically.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation requires the HTTP Request node set to POST against 'https://api.getjobber.com/api/graphql'. The payload must be a JSON object containing a 'query' string. Authentication is best handled by n8n's built-in OAuth2 credential type to manage token refreshing automatically.
